title: Notification Automation Variables
excerpt:
I have an automation set up to notify me anytime someone makes an update to a task. This is working without a problem and let’s me know which board, group and item has been updated.

posts.author: joshua.chesner (Joshua Chesner)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have an automation set up to notify me anytime someone makes an update to a task. This is working without a problem and let’s me know which board, group and item has been updated.

  1. Is there a way for it to indicate to me who made the update?
  2. Is there a way to not send the notification if the update was made by myself?

I haven’t noticed these possibilities when searching.

Thank you!

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Joshua! If you use the “User Name” auto populate field, that would include who is making the change in the notification message.

Screenshot 2024-09-09 at 2.17.05 PM896×627 33 KB

For your other point, at this time, there is not a condition that would exclude your user when it comes to not triggering the automation if you are the one who made the change.
